meta.title: Brazil vs South Korea — Round of 16

context: A pivotal moment in this round of 16 match. Brazil lead South Korea 4-1. The game hangs in the balance — every decision from the touchline could decide the outcome.

real_decision.description: Brazil won 4-1. Vinícius José Paixão de Oliveira Júnior (6'), Neymar da Silva Santos Junior (12'), Richarlison de Andrade (28'), Lucas Tolentino Coelho de Lima (35'), Seung-Ho Paik (75').

pre_generated_explanations.A:
**OPTION A — Defensive substitution — bring on a fresh center-back, sit deep**
Risk level: Medium. This is the safe, professional choice that protects the lead without abandoning attacking threat. pre_generated_explanations.B:
**OPTION B — Drop deeper as a unit, protect the lead, counter-attack**
Risk level: Low. This is a solid conservative choice that prioritizes defensive stability. Low risk of conceding, but may invite pressure. In the context of this round of 16 match between Brazil and South Korea (4-1), this option addresses the tactical challenge but requires precise execution from the players on the pitch.
pre_generated_explanations.C:
**OPTION C — Keep attacking — the best defense is a good offense**
Risk level: High. This is the aggressive choice. Keeping attacking intent when leading can catch opponents off guard but leaves defensive gaps. In the context of this round of 16 match between Brazil and South Korea (4-1), this option addresses the tactical challenge but requires precise execution from the players on the pitch.
pre_generated_explanations.D:
**OPTION D — Bring on a defensive midfielder, switch to 5-4-1**
Risk level: Low. This is the ultra-defensive option. Maximum security at the back, but surrenders possession and invites sustained pressure.
